headline: After three heatwaves, French people worry: 'Next year will be worse. How will we cope?'
description: Across France, people of all ages have realized how vulnerable they are to extreme temperatures, which have stunned the country during these past weeks.

War is undermining social contract between Putin and Russians
description: The spread of Ukrainian attacks across the country is deepening Russians' concerns about the regime's ability to protect their basic needs, as the economic situation deteriorates.

headline: 'We are trapped in a climate dystopia, a black comedy scenario, where the grotesque rivals the Orwellian farce'
description: As heatwaves multiply, public debate often amounts to little more than empty gestures. Recent government decisions to cut green funds and return energy-inefficient homes to the rental market reveal the denial prevailing among much of the political class, writes Le Monde columnist Stéphane Foucart.

headline: In a world in turmoil, 'Hamlet' has never been more relevant
description: The very embodiment of choosing chaos over building a better future, the 16th-century Shakespearean character continues to inspire contemporary theater directors.

headline: How Dakar caught surfing's new wave: 'It has become a path to success and a way of life'
description: The Senegalese coast, with its small villages and beaches where Atlantic swells create powerful waves that draw surfers from around the world, has produced a new generation of young champions eager to ride. Every day, they take on the ocean at about 12 spots along this wild, little-known shoreline.

headline: Behind the scenes of Macron's last G7, as told by photographer Kamil Zihnioglu
description: There is protocol, and then there is Donald Trump. That, in essence, is what photographer Kamil Zihnioglu took away from his three days covering the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains for Le Monde.

headline: 'It's Mad Max on the ocean': The cocaine-loaded speedboats racing to Europe's shores
description: In the Atlantic, hundreds of powerful go-fast boats, custom-built to transport tons of cocaine, retrieve shipments from vessels traveling commercial routes between South America, Africa and Europe, according to an investigation by Le Monde in partnership with NDR, WDR, Süddeutsche Zeitung, NRC, IrpiMedia and The Washington Post.
byline: Liselotte Mas,  Elsa Delmas and  Thomas Saintourens
